sections.summary: This summary provides key information about the MeshCompute utility token. It is intended to support the prospective holder in making an informed decision. The summary covers the offeror, the project, the offer to the public, the rights and obligations attached to the token, the underlying technology, the principal risks, and the environmental impact of the consensus mechanism. The full white paper must be read alongside this summary.
sections.risk_warning: The crypto-asset described in this white paper may lose its value in part or in full, may not always be transferable, and may not be liquid. Past performance of the MeshCompute token is not indicative of future performance. The crypto-asset is not covered by an investor compensation scheme or a deposit guarantee scheme. The risk of total loss is real and the holder should not invest amounts that cannot be lost in full.
sections.management_statement: The management body of the offeror declares that this white paper complies with the requirements of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 (MiCAR), that the information presented is fair, clear, and not misleading, and that no information has been omitted that would be material to the assessment of the crypto-asset. The management body assumes responsibility for the content of this white paper.
sections.notification_date: Date of notification to BaFin: 1 August 2026.
sections.language: This white paper is drawn up in English, a language customary in the sphere of international finance, and a German-language translation is available on the offeror's project portal.
sections.offeror: MeshCompute Labs UG (haftungsbeschränkt) is the offeror of the crypto-asset. MeshCompute Labs is a limited liability company organised under the laws of the Federal Republic of Germany in the legal form of an Unternehmergesellschaft (haftungsbeschränkt), with its registered seat and business address at Schanzenstraße 22, 20357 Hamburg, registered with the commercial register of the Local Court of Hamburg under HRB 198472.
sections.issuer: The issuer of the MeshCompute token is the same legal entity as the offeror. No separate issuer exists. The offeror holds the relevant rights to issue the token on the underlying protocol and is registered as described above.
sections.trading_platform_operator: Not applicable. The white paper is drawn up by the offeror. The token is not currently admitted to trading on a regulated trading platform operator.
sections.project: The MeshCompute project develops a decentralised marketplace for verifiable off-chain compute, with the purpose of allowing AI inference workloads to be priced and settled against verifiable execution proofs. The business model is based on protocol fees on settled compute, paid in the native token by buyers of compute capacity. The founding team consists of three engineers with prior experience in distributed systems, ZK proof systems, and high-throughput payments infrastructure.
sections.offer_or_admission: The offer is addressed to professional and eligible counterparty customers, and to retail customers in selected European Union jurisdictions for which the offeror has completed the passporting notification. The offer is conducted at a fixed subscription price denominated in euro, in two tranches, with a hard cap on the total amount offered. The target market includes developers and operators of compute-intensive AI workloads. The jurisdictions outside the scope of the passport are listed in Annex 1 to this white paper.
sections.crypto_asset: The MeshCompute token is a crypto-asset within the meaning of Art. 3(1)(5) MiCAR. The total supply is fixed at one billion units and is recorded in the smart contract documentation. The token may be transferred between addresses on the underlying protocol with no whitelist restriction. The functionality of the token is to settle protocol fees and to grant access rights to verifiable compute scheduling.
sections.rights_and_obligations: The holder has the contractual right to use the token to pay protocol fees on the MeshCompute marketplace and to enforce these rights against the offeror in accordance with the terms of service. The holder has no right to redemption against the offeror, no claim against the offeror's assets, and no equity-like rights. The obligations of the holder include compliance with applicable transfer restrictions and the offeror's terms of service.
sections.technology: The MeshCompute token is issued on the Ethereum mainnet, which uses a proof-of-stake consensus mechanism. The smart contract implementation has been audited by two independent code security firms. Custody of user funds is the responsibility of the user; no custody is provided by the offeror. The protocol is open source and the deployed contract address is published on the offeror's portal.
sections.risks: The principal risks include market risk on the token price, project risk including failure of the marketplace to attract sufficient supply or demand, technology risk including smart contract failure and protocol-level bugs, regulatory risk including future changes in MiCAR or AML/CFT requirements, liquidity risk including the absence of a deep secondary market, and operational risk including infrastructure outages. Each risk is described in detail in the dedicated risk section of this white paper, together with mitigation measures.
sections.environmental_impact: The consensus mechanism of the underlying protocol is proof-of-stake, which consumes substantially less energy than proof-of-work consensus mechanisms. The offeror monitors the energy and climate-related impact metrics for the underlying protocol as published by independent third parties and publishes the annual environmental and climate-related impact data in line with the ESMA regulatory technical standards on sustainability indicators for distributed ledger technology.
